## [09:05 - 12:06]

I was also the smallest kid, believe it or not. You know, I grew 10 inches my junior year in high school. So I was five one my sophomore year to give you an idea. And I would also get picked on. You can buy your own clothes. It's a different world. But the point is that that same experience, like I'm being fearful. The first step for me was I had to fake it to survive. And there's an interesting thing about your body when you fake like you fake laugh and you laugh and laugh and you fake laugh. You know, some people do this sometimes just see what happens. And what happens is your nervous system gets a trigger where it actually ignites and you really begin to laugh. Same thing is true with confidence. We've all heard fake it to you make it. So I think my first approach was kind of fake it to you make it. But that isn't a very lasting approach. It's a good like bandaid for the moment. And it was like, you know, how do I get through this situation? So this person doesn't end up, you know, taking me down in a fight. I'm happy. I'm happy. But, you know, I wasn't even slightly happy. And I began to realize that affirmations without discipline were the beginning of delusion that I needed to affirm things. But I needed to do something that would increase that confidence. Right. You know, I needed to, I needed to like learn how to fight, fight, learn how to protect myself, not just say, I'm strong. I can handle these guys. I mean, that's, that's an illusion. And so I began to do a combination of things. I did things for my psychology to build more confidence because I realized that affirmation was enough. So I started calling it an incantation because I remember studying, you know, these magicians, you know, you read the mythology of these magicians, and they would say these things over and over again with such intensity and they'd turn a frog into a prince, prince of the frog. And I thought, you know what, I'm saying I'm happy. I'm happy. But I'm not feeling it. I'm not using my body. My face isn't saying it. So I would do this incantation in my car, literally my 1968 Volkswagen bug. And I'm driving from East Los Angeles to the place called the Sandgearber Valley to San Fernando Valley. It's been an hour drive. And I'd be screaming, I now command my subconscious mind to directly helping this person to change their life today. If I give me the strength, the emotion, the persuasion, the humor, the brevity, whatever it takes to show this person and get this person to change their life now. I don't even know that so clearly because I did it for years. But I would get in this car and do that for an hour drive. And if you were driving by me on the freeway, you probably thought I was a serial killer screaming the shit at the top of my lungs. But whenever two people meet, if there's rapport, if there's respect, you've got to have rapport and respect. The person who's most certain will influence the other person. And I would sit down with this person and they were like, you know, I'm not going to do this. I said, no, you understand. You wouldn't have come here to meet me if you hadn't already believed that this was valuable and you want to go and you're going to go.

## [12:06 - 15:06]

And they would look at me like I was insane to be like, these aren't the droids you're looking for. Because my certainty was so strong and it came from wanting to serve them. It came from a place of love. But I didn't have confidence. I had such absolute certainty and connection that literally when I worked for Jim Rohn, every person I met for the first two months, I convinced him to his programs. These are people who said they couldn't do it as a possible do it. I came to the top guy in his company and it was all building the certainty. I used to do the same thing around economics where, again, I wasn't just affirming it. I was doing this incantation at the top of my lungs for hours until I believed it. I owned it. I experienced it. But then I went also worked on the skills. Whatever skill was necessary, I teamed it so I have confidence and confidence. And I can't share that enough because I'll tell you one of the worst things you can do. The worst thing you can do is take your child and I know this not based on anecdotal, my own personal experience. I know this based on the research that's been done. Take your child and constantly praise them thinking that's going to make them confident. Especially if you're praising them like for their intelligence or their talent or their skill. And the reason is there's a woman from Stanford University for psychology there named Carol Dweck. And she did these 18 years studies and 11 years intensively where she found the impact upon kids. When you teach kids and most of us have done this, oh, you're so beautiful. Oh, you're so smart. Oh, you're so skilled. What happens is they love it in the moment. But then what happens is when they have a difficulty, when all of a sudden it's not so easy, it doesn't flow so well, they start saying, oh my God, maybe I'm not that smart. And it scares them. It scares them unconsciously. And then they don't think they have the ability. In fact, one of her studies showed that kids that were taught that they're so smart, when they were given a difficult test, 40% lied about their score when they're asked about what their score was. 40%. Because it's inconsistent with their identity. And the way to really praise, the way to build real confidence is to praise your kids for their efforts. Say, wow, that took an unbelievable amount of creativity. You probably tried 20 different ways to get that done. That's incredible. Praising effort, praising variability, praising someone who's persistent. Those are the qualities that will make them be confident long term because with those skill sets, they'll be able to succeed. So I'm not a believer in confidence for just confidence's sake. I believe confidence is the door opener. But you've got to make sure that behind that door opener, you've got the core skill sets. And the core skill sets are not just how to do it. Like I said, I may not know how to do it, but I'm confident I'm going to figure it out. Because I'm a persistent son of a bitch. Because I'm determined, because I'm resourceful, because I'm creative, because I'm flexible. And those are all qualities that are ongoing. You have things that you can do to increase their quality. And you're all those things. I'm not blowing smoke your way. You know who you are. When people look at you and they might say, you're lucky. You're the furthest thing from lucky on the planet. I'm excited for people to see your movie coming out, your story of your life.
